(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社}} (佐賀縣護國神社[[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Saga (city)|Saga]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) | established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga City buildings and structures]] [[Category:Saga City history]] == See Also == * Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Saga Gokoku (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社}} (佐賀縣護國神社[[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Saga (city)|Saga]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) | established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Saga Gokoku Shrine|Saga Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社}} (佐賀縣護國神社[[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Saga (city)|Saga]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) | established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga City buildings and structures]] [[Category:Saga City history]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1870 establishments]] in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社}} (佐賀縣護國神社[[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Saga (city)|Saga]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) | established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) | established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga City buildings and structures]] [[Category:Saga City history]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Saga Gokoku Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[en:draft:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Saga Gokoku Shrine|Saga Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社}} (佐賀縣護國神社[[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Saga (city)|Saga]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) | established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga City buildings and structures]] [[Category:Saga City history]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1870 establishments]] {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[en:draft:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Saga Gokoku Shrine|Saga Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社}} (佐賀縣護國神社[[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Saga (city)|Saga]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) | established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga City buildings and structures]] [[Category:Saga City history]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Saga Gokoku The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga City buildings and structures]] [[Category:Saga City history]] == See Also == * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) | established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga City buildings and structures]] [[Category:Saga City history]] == See Also == * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) | established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga City buildings and structures]] [[Category:Saga City history]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Saga Gokoku {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[en:draft:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Saga Gokoku Shrine|Saga Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社}} (佐賀縣護國神社[[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Saga (city)|Saga]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) | established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga City buildings and structures]] [[Category:Saga City history]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1870 establishments]] [[Category:Military monuments and memorials]] [[Category:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:Unsorted]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on [[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] }} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社}} (佐賀縣護國神社[[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Saga (city)|Saga]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) | established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga City buildings and structures]] [[Category:Saga City history]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Saga Gokoku Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[en:draft:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Saga Gokoku Shrine|Saga Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社}} (佐賀縣護國神社[[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Saga (city)|Saga]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) | established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga City buildings and structures]] [[Category:Saga City history]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1870 establishments]] It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社}} (佐賀縣護國神社[[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Saga (city)|Saga]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) | established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga City buildings and structures]] [[Category:Saga City history]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1870 establishments]] in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[en:draft:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Saga Gokoku Shrine|Saga Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社}} (佐賀縣護國神社[[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Saga (city)|Saga]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) | established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga City buildings and structures]] [[Category:Saga City history]] == See Also! == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1870 establishments]] Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) | established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga City buildings and structures]] [[Category:Saga City history]] == See Also == *
