Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Saga Gokoku (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社}} (佐賀縣護國神社[[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Saga (city)|Saga]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) | established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[en:draft:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Saga Gokoku Shrine|Saga Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社}} (佐賀縣護國神社[[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Saga (city)|Saga]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) | established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga City buildings and structures]] [[Category:Saga City history]] == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1870 establishments]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社}} (佐賀縣護國神社[[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]]) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Saga (city)|Saga]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) | established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga City buildings and structures]] [[Category:Saga City history]] == See Also == * Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * (Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社}} (佐賀縣護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Saga (city)|Saga]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine)|established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|12=draft}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市|12=draft}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saga Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社}} (佐賀縣護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Saga (city)|Saga]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine)|established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|12=draft}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市|12=draft}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saga Gokoku Shrine]] }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga City buildings and structures]] [[Category:Saga City history]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1870 establishments]] [[Category:Military monuments and memorials]] Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|12=draft}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市|12=draft}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} == See The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社}} (佐賀縣護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Saga (city)|Saga]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine)|established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|12=draft}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市|12=draft}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saga Gokoku Shrine]] }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] for the It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine)|established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|12=draft}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市|12=draft}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saga Gokoku Shrine]] }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga City buildings and structures]] [[Category:Saga City history]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1870 establishments]] [[Category:Military monuments and memorials]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]]}} [[en:draft:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]] [[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] {{citation needed|Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}

The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saga {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Saga Gokoku Shrine|Saga Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社}} (佐賀縣護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Saga (city)|Saga]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine)|established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saga Gokoku Shrine]] }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Saga City buildings and structures]] [[Category:Saga City history]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:1870 establishments]] [[Category:Military monuments and memorials]] [[Category:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]] [[en:Saga Gokoku Shrine]]

revision.comment: 13 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:佐賀縣護國神社 up to rev 102019916
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:08 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称=佐賀縣護國神社 | 画像=[[Image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[佐賀県]][[佐賀市]]川原町8-15 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=国事殉難者
殉職自衛官 | 社格=内務大臣指定[[護国神社]]・[[別表神社]] | 創建=[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]） | 例祭=[[4月13日]] - [[4月15日|15日]]
[[10月13日]] - [[10月15日|15日]] | 地図={{Location map|Japan Saga|label=佐賀縣
護國神社|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''佐賀縣護國神社'''（さがけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[佐賀県]][[佐賀市]]川原町にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。 [[明治維新]]以降の国難に殉じた佐賀県関係の戦没者および[[第二次世界大戦]]後の殉職[[自衛官]]、あわせて約3万5千柱を祀る。 == 歴史 == [[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）、旧[[佐賀藩]]主・[[鍋島直大]]が、[[戊辰戦争]]で戦死した藩士78柱を祀ったことに始まる。明治7年以降、[[佐賀の乱]]などの戦死者を合祀した。当初は「招魂場」と称していたが、明治8年に官祭[[招魂社]]となり、昭和14年、招魂社の制度改正により内務大臣指定の佐賀縣護國神社に改称した。第二次世界大戦後は昭和22年より肥前神社と称していたが、日本の主権回復後の昭和27年に元の佐賀縣護國神社に復した。 == 画像 == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|太鼓橋と楠 File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|みたままつりの[[灯籠流し]]（神社東の[[多布施川]]） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さかけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:佐賀県の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:佐賀市の建築物]] [[Category:佐賀市の歴史]]

Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saga The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{citation needed|Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|佐賀縣護國神社|version=102019916|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[every:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]] [[en:Saga Gokoku Shrine]]

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社}} (佐賀縣護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Saga (city)|Saga]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image =
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine)|established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga City|ja|佐賀市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{citation needed|Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|佐賀縣護國神社|version=102019916|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[every:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]] [[en:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|佐賀縣護國神社|version=102019916|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[every:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]] [[en:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saga Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (佐賀縣護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Saga (city)|Saga]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image =
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} (Gokoku Shrine)|established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in [[:en:Saga (city)|Saga City]], Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|佐賀縣護國神社|version=102019916|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[every:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]] [[en:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]]

Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} (Gokoku Shrine)|established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in [[:en:Saga (city)|Saga City]], Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|佐賀縣護國神社|version=102019916|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[every:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[en:draft:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]] [[en:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Saga Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:佐賀市の建築物]] [[Category:佐賀市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saga Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (佐賀縣護國神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Saga (city)|Saga]], [[Japan]]. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of [[Yasukuni Shrine]]. They were renamed from [[Shōkonsha|Shokonsha]] in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image =
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} (Gokoku Shrine)|established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in [[:en:Saga (city)|Saga City]], Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the [[Japan Self-Defense Forces]] who died in the line of duty after [[World War II]]. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by [[Nabeshima Naomasa]], the former lord of the [[Saga Domain]], to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the [[Boshin War]]. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the [[Saga Rebellion]] and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * [[Martyrs' shrines (China)|Martyr's Shrines]] * [[:en:Martial temple|Martial temple]] and [[:en:Wen Wu temple|Wen Wu temple]] * [[National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine]] * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[:en:Chinese Cultural Renaissance|Chinese Cultural Renaissance]] * [[Ancestral shrine]] * [[:en:Gallant Garden|Gallant Garden]] * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * [[:en:Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier]] * [[:en:Arlington National Cemetery|Arlington National Cemetery]] * [[:en:Valhalla|Valhalla]] (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * [[:en:Walhalla (memorial)|Walhalla Shrine]] (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * [[Eternal Spring Shrine]] * [[The common end of myriad good deeds]] * [[:en:Greek hero cult|Greek hero cult]] == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please
