[[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]] [[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]] [[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11384878]] Wikidata name: Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to {{ill|Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion|en|Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion|WD=Q13429672|lt=Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion}} to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Saga Gokoku Shrine|Saga Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saga Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (佐賀縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Saga (city)|en|Saga (city)|WD=Q209766|lt=Saga}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image =
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} (Gokoku Shrine)|established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|qq=draft}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga (city)|en|Saga (city)|WD=Q209766|lt=Saga City}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the {{ill|Japan Self-Defense Forces|en|Japan Self-Defense Forces|WD=Q275488|lt=Japan Self-Defense Forces}} who died in the line of duty after {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by {{ill|Nabeshima Naomasa|en|Nabeshima Naomasa|WD=Q3334677|lt=Nabeshima Naomasa}}, the former lord of the {{ill|Saga Domain|en|Saga Domain|WD=Q2975995|lt=Saga Domain}}, to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the {{ill|Saga Rebellion|en|Saga Rebellion|WD=Q2446765|lt=Saga Rebellion}} and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|WD=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple}} and {{ill|Wen Wu temple|en|Wen Wu temple|WD=Q7231588|lt=Wen Wu temple}} * {{ill|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|en|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|WD=Q5956488|lt=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine}} * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|en|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|WD=Q1073969|lt=Chinese Cultural Renaissance}} * [[Ancestral shrine]] * {{ill|Gallant Garden|en|Gallant Garden|WD=Q5518867|lt=Gallant Garden}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|WD=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|WD=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|WD=Q177860|lt=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * {{ill|Walhalla (memorial)|en|Walhalla (memorial)|WD=Q321240|lt=Walhalla Shrine}} (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|The common end of myriad good deeds|en|The common end of myriad good deeds|WD=Q9609095|lt=The common end of myriad good deeds}} * {{ill|Greek hero cult|en|Greek hero cult|WD=Q1878906|lt=Greek hero cult}} == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|佐賀縣護國神社|version=102019916|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{everybodywiki link|Saga Gokoku Shrine}} [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]] [[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11384878}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Saga]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} == See revision.text:
[[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]] [[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]] [[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]] {{afc comment|1=to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Saga Gokoku Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Saga Gokoku Shrine|Saga Gokoku Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saga Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (佐賀縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Saga (city)|en|Saga (city)|WD=Q209766|lt=Saga}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image =
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} (Gokoku Shrine)|established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|qq=draft}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga (city)|en|Saga (city)|WD=Q209766|lt=Saga City}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the {{ill|Japan Self-Defense Forces|en|Japan Self-Defense Forces|WD=Q275488|lt=Japan Self-Defense Forces}} who died in the line of duty after {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by {{ill|Nabeshima Naomasa|en|Nabeshima Naomasa|WD=Q3334677|lt=Nabeshima Naomasa}}, the former lord of the {{ill|Saga Domain|en|Saga Domain|WD=Q2975995|lt=Saga Domain}}, to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the {{ill|Saga Rebellion|en|Saga Rebellion|WD=Q2446765|lt=Saga Rebellion}} and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|WD=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple}} and {{ill|Wen Wu temple|en|Wen Wu temple|WD=Q7231588|lt=Wen Wu temple}} * {{ill|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|en|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|WD=Q5956488|lt=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine}} * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|en|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|WD=Q1073969|lt=Chinese Cultural Renaissance}} * [[Ancestral shrine]] * {{ill|Gallant Garden|en|Gallant Garden|WD=Q5518867|lt=Gallant Garden}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|WD=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|WD=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|WD=Q177860|lt=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * {{ill|Walhalla (memorial)|en|Walhalla (memorial)|WD=Q321240|lt=Walhalla Shrine}} (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|The common end of myriad good deeds|en|The common end of myriad good deeds|WD=Q9609095|lt=The common end of myriad good deeds}} * {{ill|Greek hero cult|en|Greek hero cult|WD=Q1878906|lt=Greek hero cult}} == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} }} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|佐賀縣護國神社|version=102019916|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{everybodywiki link|Saga Gokoku Shrine}} [[image:Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG|250px]] [[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11384878}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Saga]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]] [[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]] [[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saga Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (佐賀縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Saga (city)|en|Saga (city)|WD=Q209766|lt=Saga|1=Saga (city)}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha|1=Shōkonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image =
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} (Gokoku Shrine)|established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|qq=draft}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga (city)|en|Saga (city)|WD=Q209766|lt=Saga City|1=Saga (city)}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the {{ill|Japan Self-Defense Forces|en|Japan Self-Defense Forces|WD=Q275488|lt=Japan Self-Defense Forces|1=Japan Self-Defense Forces}} who died in the line of duty after {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by {{ill|Nabeshima Naomasa|en|Nabeshima Naomasa|WD=Q3334677|lt=Nabeshima Naomasa|1=Nabeshima Naomasa}}, the former lord of the {{ill|Saga Domain|en|Saga Domain|WD=Q2975995|lt=Saga Domain|1=Saga Domain}}, to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the {{ill|Saga Rebellion|en|Saga Rebellion|WD=Q2446765|lt=Saga Rebellion|1=Saga Rebellion}} and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines|1=Martyrs' shrines (China)}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|WD=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple|1=Martial temple}} and {{ill|Wen Wu temple|en|Wen Wu temple|WD=Q7231588|lt=Wen Wu temple|1=Wen Wu temple}} * {{ill|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|en|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|WD=Q5956488|lt=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|1=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine}} * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine|1=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|en|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|WD=Q1073969|lt=Chinese Cultural Renaissance|1=Chinese Cultural Renaissance}} * [[Ancestral shrine]] * {{ill|Gallant Garden|en|Gallant Garden|WD=Q5518867|lt=Gallant Garden|1=Gallant Garden}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|WD=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|1=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|WD=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery|1=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|WD=Q177860|lt=Valhalla|1=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * {{ill|Walhalla (memorial)|en|Walhalla (memorial)|WD=Q321240|lt=Walhalla Shrine|1=Walhalla (memorial)}} (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine|1=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|The common end of myriad good deeds|en|The common end of myriad good deeds|WD=Q9609095|lt=The common end of myriad good deeds|1=The common end of myriad good deeds}} * {{ill|Greek hero cult|en|Greek hero cult|WD=Q1878906|lt=Greek hero cult|1=Greek hero cult}} == Notes == {{Notelist}} == revision.text:
[[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]] [[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]] [[ja:佐賀縣護國神社]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Sagaken Gokaku Jinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = }} {{ill|Saga Gokoku Shrine|ja|佐賀縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saga Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} (佐賀縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Saga (city)|en|Saga (city)|WD=Q209766|lt=Saga|1=Saga (city)}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|WD=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha|1=Shōkonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} ==ja== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image =
Worship Hall | location = Kawaramachi 8-15, Saga City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|region:JP-41|display=inline,title}} | deity = Spirits of national service
Fallen Self-Defense Forces members | type = {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} (Gokoku Shrine)|established = Meiji 3 (1870) | festivals = April 13–15
October 13–15 | map = {{location map|Japan Saga|label=Saga Prefecture
Gokoku Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E|qq=draft}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|15|10|N|130|17|35|E}}}} }} '''Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (佐賀縣護國神社, Saga-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} (Gokoku Shrine) located in {{ill|Saga (city)|en|Saga (city)|WD=Q209766|lt=Saga City|1=Saga (city)}}, Saga Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines approximately 35,000 spirits related to Saga Prefecture, including those who fell in national service since the [[Meiji Restoration]] and members of the {{ill|Japan Self-Defense Forces|en|Japan Self-Defense Forces|WD=Q275488|lt=Japan Self-Defense Forces|1=Japan Self-Defense Forces}} who died in the line of duty after {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}. == History == The shrine was founded in 1870 (Meiji 3) by {{ill|Nabeshima Naomasa|en|Nabeshima Naomasa|WD=Q3334677|lt=Nabeshima Naomasa|1=Nabeshima Naomasa}}, the former lord of the {{ill|Saga Domain|en|Saga Domain|WD=Q2975995|lt=Saga Domain|1=Saga Domain}}, to honor 78 warriors of the Saga Domain who fell during the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}. From 1874 (Meiji 7) onwards, it also enshrined those who died in the {{ill|Saga Rebellion|en|Saga Rebellion|WD=Q2446765|lt=Saga Rebellion|1=Saga Rebellion}} and other conflicts. Initially called "Shōkonjō" (招魂場), it became an official government-sponsored "Shōkonsha" (招魂社) in 1875 (Meiji 8). In 1939 (Shōwa 14), the shrine was renamed Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine under a government reorganization of Shōkonsha into Gokoku Shrines. Following Japan’s defeat in World War II, it was renamed Hizen Shrine in 1947 (Shōwa 22). With the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the original name, Saga Prefecture Gokoku Shrine, was reinstated. == Gallery == File:Arched bridge and camphor trees at Gokoku Shrine in Saga.jpg|Arched bridge and camphor trees File:Toro Nagashi at Tafuse River Saga 2017.jpg|Lantern floating ceremony (Toro Nagashi) during the Mitama festival at Tafuse River {{Shinto-stub}} == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|WD=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines|1=Martyrs' shrines (China)}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|WD=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple|1=Martial temple}} and {{ill|Wen Wu temple|en|Wen Wu temple|WD=Q7231588|lt=Wen Wu temple|1=Wen Wu temple}} * {{ill|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|en|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|WD=Q5956488|lt=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|1=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine}} * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine|1=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|en|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|WD=Q1073969|lt=Chinese Cultural Renaissance|1=Chinese Cultural Renaissance}} * [[Ancestral shrine]] * {{ill|Gallant Garden|en|Gallant Garden|WD=Q5518867|lt=Gallant Garden|1=Gallant Garden}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|WD=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|1=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|WD=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery|1=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|WD=Q177860|lt=Valhalla|1=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * {{ill|Walhalla (memorial)|en|Walhalla (memorial)|WD=Q321240|lt=Walhalla Shrine|1=Walhalla (memorial)}} (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|WD=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine|1=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|The common end of myriad good deeds|en|The common end of myriad good deeds|WD=Q9609095|lt=The common end of myriad good deeds|1=The common end of myriad good deeds}} * {{ill|Greek hero cult|en|Greek hero cult|WD=Q1878906|lt=Greek hero cult|1=Greek hero cult}} == Notes == {{Notelist}} ==
